nodes.name: Send Email: Send Email 1 (Thank You + Proposal Reference)

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "What we saw at your home today, {{contact.first_name}}", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
Plus a few things worth knowing before you review your proposal ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

Thanks for having {{contact.rep_display_name}} out to your home today. We know a 90-minute assessment is a real commitment, and we do not take that lightly.

I'll check in with you over the next couple of days. My role here at Reece is simple, to make sure homeowners have everything they need to make a clear decision after their appointment — no pressure, just clarity. {{contact.rep_display_name}} is still your main point of contact, but I stay involved behind the scenes to make sure nothing gets missed.

When {{contact.rep_first_name}} walked through your home and showed you where the exposure points are, you probably noticed something most homeowners miss from the outside, those specific openings that drive your home's entire protection classification. That is exactly why we do this in person. No online tool catches what was found at your property today.

Your proposal reflects exactly what your home needs. Not more, not less.

A few things worth knowing as you review it:

— Every product in your proposal meets or exceeds the Florida Building Code requirements for your specific zone.
— The pricing includes full installation, permitting, HOA documentation, and a warranty that actually covers what matters.
— Financing options are available that often make this project cash-flow positive when you factor in the insurance and energy savings.

If anything from today raised a question you did not get to ask, just reply to this email or reach out to {{contact.rep_first_name}} directly. Either way, we will get you an answer.

Out of everything you went over with {{contact.rep_first_name}}, what stood out most to you?

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

(Working with {{contact.rep_display_name}})

P.S. Randy Reece's father started this company to protect families from storms. That was 1972. Every home we assess gets the same care as if it were our own.
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.city}}, FL
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Check if Active Entry is Canvassing #2
Send Email: Send Email 2: Social Proof Story

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "She almost waited — until she saw what it actually returned", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
A {{contact.city}} homeowner who had the same reaction you probably did ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

After {{contact.rep_first_name}} left your place yesterday, I thought about a homeowner we worked with last year in a neighborhood similar to yours.

Same situation: older windows, mixed opening sizes, a few code concerns that were not obvious until we measured.

She had the same reaction you might have had when {{contact.rep_first_name}} showed you what was behind your walls — “I knew it was not great, but I did not know it was THAT exposed.”

That is usually the moment homeowners pause, because they realize it was not just “old windows.” It was something that could turn into a real problem faster than she expected.

Here is what happened after her installation:

Her insurance company reclassified her home. Her annual premium dropped by over $2,400.

Her energy bills came down $140 a month within the first billing cycle.

When she refinanced six months later, the appraiser noted the impact window upgrade as a material improvement. Her home value increased by more than what she paid for the project.

She told us: “I kept putting it off because I thought it was an expense. It turned out to be the best investment I have made in this house.”

What surprised her was not just the savings. It was realizing how close she had been to a major issue she did not even know existed, and how straightforward the fix turned out to be.

I am not saying your numbers will be identical. Every home is different, and that is exactly why we do personalized assessments instead of one-size-fits-all quotes.

But the pattern is consistent. Homeowners who protect their homes properly tend to recover the investment faster than they expected.

Your proposal is built the same way. Specific to your home, your zone, your needs.

This is exactly why I stay involved after the appointment, to make sure that moment doesn't get lost once things settle.

If you have questions, I am here — or you can reach out to {{contact.rep_first_name}} directly.

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

(Working with {{contact.rep_display_name}})
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.city}}, FL
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Send Email: Send Email 3: Why Homeowners Wait

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: ""We need to think about it" — here's what that actually means", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
You’re not the first homeowner to say this after an assessment ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

After an appointment like the one {{contact.rep_first_name}} just had at your home, almost every homeowner says the same thing:

“We just need to think about it.”

And that makes complete sense. It is your home. It is a real commitment. Nobody should rush a decision like this.

But here is something I have noticed after working alongside our assessment team for a long time:

“Thinking about it” rarely means they need more information. They already have the proposal. They already saw the numbers. They already stood in their own home and watched {{contact.rep_first_name}} point out the exact openings that matter.

What “thinking about it” usually means is that the size of the decision has not caught up to the clarity they felt during the appointment.

In that moment, when {{contact.rep_first_name}} was walking through your home and you could see exactly what was being talked about, it made sense. The problem was clear. The fix was clear. The investment made sense in context.

Then you sit with it for a day, and the clarity starts to compete with the number.

That is not a sign something is wrong. That is how every homeowner processes a meaningful investment.

The homeowners who move forward tend to do one thing: they go back to the proposal, look at the insurance and energy savings section, and run the math against what they are paying now. When they see the gap, what they are quietly losing every month by keeping things the way they are — the decision reframes itself.

It stops being “Should we spend this?” and becomes “How long are we comfortable losing this?”

Your proposal has those numbers. They are specific to your home, your zone, and your current classification.

If you want us to walk through that section together, just reply or call. Takes about five minutes.

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

(Working with {{contact.rep_display_name}})
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.city}}, FL
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Check if Active Entry is Canvassing #3
Send SMS: Send SMS 2: Decision framing
Send Email: Send Email 4: Common Questions

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "The 4 questions you probably have right now", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
After 50 years doing this, we’ve noticed a pattern ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

These are the questions homeowners ask after they have seen what we saw together at their home.

After doing this for over 50 years as a company, we have noticed a pattern. Almost every homeowner asks the same four questions within a week of their assessment. Here they are:

1. “Is this the right time, or should I wait?”

The honest answer: the best time was before hurricane season. The second-best time is now. Material costs trend upward. Insurance companies are tightening classification windows. And Florida Building Code requirements only get stricter, not looser. Waiting does not make the project cheaper or easier.

2. “How does financing work?”

Most of our homeowners use one of our financing options that spread the project over several months. When you factor in insurance savings and energy reduction, many projects are close to cash-flow neutral from month one. We can run the exact numbers for your proposal.

3. “What about my HOA?”

We handle HOA documentation and approval as part of the project. This is not an add-on or extra cost. Our team has worked with most of the major HOAs in {{contact.city}} and we know exactly what they need.

4. “How long does installation take?”

For a project like yours, typical installation is 1–2 days depending on scope. We coordinate everything: permitting, scheduling, crew assignment, final inspection. You do not manage any of it.

If your question is not on this list, reply to this email or reach out to {{contact.rep_first_name}}. Either one of us will get you an answer.

At this point, most homeowners are not deciding if they will do this — just when.

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

(Working with {{contact.rep_display_name}})

P.S. Randy’s dad used to say, “The best contractor is the one who answers the question you were afraid to ask.” That is what we are here for.
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.city}}, FL
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Send Email: Send Email 5: Urgency + ROI + Identity

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "What your home may be quietly costing you each month", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
The math most window companies will never share with you ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

I want to share something with you that most window companies never talk about.

Every month your home sits with unrated or underrated openings, three things are happening:

1. Your insurance company is classifying your home in a risk tier that costs you hundreds more per year than necessary. That money is gone, you do not get it back when you eventually upgrade.

2. Your energy costs are higher than they need to be. According to the U.S. Department of Energy, upgrading to ENERGY STAR certified windows saves 7 to 15 percent on annual energy bills, which typically works out to $200 to $600 per year in most homes.

In South Florida, where cooling runs most of the year, that gap adds up faster than most homeowners expect.

3. Your home’s appraised value does not reflect the upgrade. When the time comes to refinance, sell, or pass this home to your kids, that gap matters.

Especially as we get closer to the next storm season, that gap between where your home is now and where it could be becomes harder to ignore.

We ran the numbers in your proposal. The combined savings from insurance reclassification + energy reduction often offset the monthly financing payment within the first year.

As a company, we have seen this pattern across hundreds of homes in {{contact.city}}:

The homeowners who move forward tend to have one thing in common. It is not that they had more money, or fewer concerns, or a simpler situation. It is that they decided they would rather know their family is protected than wonder about it for another hurricane season.

That is not a financial decision. That is a clarity decision. And you already have the clarity, {{contact.rep_first_name}} built it with you at your kitchen table.

Either way, you are in a good position — you now understand your home in a way most people never do.

If you want us to walk through the exact savings projection for your home, just reply or call us directly. You can also reach {{contact.rep_display_name}} anytime.

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

(Working with {{contact.rep_display_name}})
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.city}}, FL
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}
